title: Wood-Telle Open Question (linear crossing number)
statement_text: Is it true that for every graph $H$ there is a constant $c = c(H)$ such that every $H$-minor-free graph $G$ has crossing number $\mathrm{cr}(G) \leq c \cdot \Delta(G) \cdot |G|$?
context_text: Wood and Telle proved the bound $\mathrm{cr}(G) \leq c\,\Delta(G)^2 \cdot |G|$ for $H$-minor-free graphs (Theorem 2 of the present paper) and left open whether the quadratic dependence on $\Delta(G)$ could be reduced to linear. The $\Omega(\Delta n)$ lower bound was already known from Pach and T\'{o}th for toroidal graphs, so a matching upper bound would be tight.
paper_contribution: Fully answers the question affirmatively by proving Theorem 3: for every graph $H$ there exists $c = c(H)$ such that every $H$-minor-free graph $G$ satisfies $\mathrm{cr}(G) \leq c\,\Delta(G) \cdot |G|$, and extends the matching $\Omega(\Delta n)$ lower bound to $K_{3,3}$-minor-free and $K_h$-minor-free graphs.
notes: Described in the abstract as 'an open question of Wood and Telle [New York J. Mathematics, 2007]'; no labelled conjecture/question environment in the source. Statement reconstructed from context since the paper does not quote it verbatim.
paper_title: Tight Upper Bounds on the Crossing Number in a Minor-Closed Class

title: Wood-Telle Conjecture (O(∑ deg²) crossing number)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there is a constant $c = c(H)$ such that every $H$-minor-free graph $G$ has crossing number $\mathrm{cr}(G) \leq c \sum_{v \in V(G)} \deg(v)^2$.
context_text: Wood and Telle conjectured this stronger bound, which would match the result already known for graphs of bounded Euler genus (Theorem 1, due to Pach–T\'{o}th and B\"or\"oczky et al.). After the paper proves the $O(\Delta(G)\cdot|G|)$ upper bound (Theorem 3), establishing the $O(\sum_v \deg(v)^2)$ bound for all excluded-minor families remains open since $\sum_v \deg(v)^2 \leq \Delta(G)\cdot 2|E(G)|$ makes it strictly stronger.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $K_5$-minor-free graphs (Section 4) and proves the analogous bound $\overline{\mathrm{cr}}(G) = O\!\left(\sum_{v} \deg(v)^2\right)$ on the rectilinear crossing number of $K_{3,3}$-minor-free graphs; the general case remains open.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction without a labelled conjecture environment: 'Wood and Telle [66] conjectured that every graph excluding a fixed minor has crossing number $O(\sum_v \deg(v)^2)$.' PDF source — math notation may be partially garbled.
paper_title: Tight Upper Bounds on the Crossing Number in a Minor-Closed Class
